title: Compressed Air System Energy Cost Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool to calculate total energy cost of compressed air systems, identify hidden losses, and suggest efficiency improvements based on ISO 50001, Lean, and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Compressor Motor Power Rating
inputs.businessContext: Nameplate power of the compressor motor. Typically from 5 kW to 5000 kW for industrial units.

businessContext_i18n.en: Nameplate power of the compressor motor. Typically from 5 kW to 5000 kW for industrial units.

inputs.label: Motor Efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Efficiency of the electric motor driving the compressor. Premium efficiency motors (IE3/IE4) are >90%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Efficiency of the electric motor driving the compressor. Premium efficiency motors (IE3/IE4) are >90%.

inputs.label: Compressor Type
inputs.businessContext: Different compressor types have different specific power curves and maintenance profiles.

businessContext_i18n.en: Different compressor types have different specific power curves and maintenance profiles.

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Total annual operating hours. Typical industrial: 8000 hours (2 shifts, 5 days).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total annual operating hours. Typical industrial: 8000 hours (2 shifts, 5 days).

inputs.label: Load Factor (Average % of Full Load)
inputs.businessContext: Average percentage of full load capacity at which the compressor operates. Includes part-load and unloaded periods.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average percentage of full load capacity at which the compressor operates. Includes part-load and unloaded periods.

inputs.label: Electricity Cost
inputs.businessContext: Blended average cost per kWh including demand charges. Typical range $0.05-$0.30.

businessContext_i18n.en: Blended average cost per kWh including demand charges. Typical range $0.05-$0.30.

inputs.label: Estimated System Leakage
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of compressed air lost through leaks. Typical industrial systems: 20-30%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of compressed air lost through leaks. Typical industrial systems: 20-30%.

inputs.label: System Pressure Setpoint
inputs.businessContext: Operating pressure at the compressor discharge. Higher pressure increases energy consumption by ~1% per 0.1 bar.

businessContext_i18n.en: Operating pressure at the compressor discharge. Higher pressure increases energy consumption by ~1% per 0.1 bar.

inputs.label: Average Ambient Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Average ambient temperature at compressor intake. Higher temperature reduces air density and increases power.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average ambient temperature at compressor intake. Higher temperature reduces air density and increases power.

inputs.label: Variable Speed Drive (VSD) Installed
inputs.businessContext: VSD can reduce energy consumption by 15-35% under part-load conditions.

businessContext_i18n.en: VSD can reduce energy consumption by 15-35% under part-load conditions.

inputs.label: Maintenance Quality Level
inputs.businessContext: Maintenance quality affects efficiency, leakage, and pressure drop. Poor maintenance can increase energy use by 10-20%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Maintenance quality affects efficiency, leakage, and pressure drop. Poor maintenance can increase energy use by 10-20%.

rules.message: With VSD, load factor above 85% may indicate oversizing. Consider fixed-speed alternative for better ROI.
High leakage combined with poor maintenance suggests urgent leak detection program.
Pressure above 10 bar(g) is uncommon for general industrial use. Verify actual requirement.

thresholds.alert: Annual energy cost exceeds $50,000. Consider energy audit and optimization.
Leakage above 30% is critical. Immediate leak repair can save up to 40% of energy.
Motor efficiency below 85%. Consider replacing with IE4 premium motor.
